generated_from.requested_focus: Reduce abandonment when saving a task fails

evidence.observation: The task editor exposes a generic save failure message with no preserved recovery action.
evidence.relevance: Users cannot tell whether their draft is safe or how to complete the interrupted job.

evidence.observation: Users report retyping task details after intermittent save failures.
evidence.relevance: The failure creates repeated work and undermines trust in the editor.

evidence.observation: A local draft store already supports temporary persistence for unsent content.
evidence.relevance: The existing persistence seam can support recovery without a new storage subsystem.

evidence.observation: Task creation is likely a frequent workflow for active collaborators.
evidence.relevance: Frequency increases the potential value, but analytics should confirm the assumption.

decision.selection_rationale: Guided recovery directly protects user work, reuses an existing draft seam, and provides a measurable reduction in failed-save abandonment without expanding the core task model.

candidates.title: Guided Error Recovery
candidates.problem: Users lose confidence and may repeat work when a task save fails without a clear recovery path.
candidates.measurable_outcome: Reduce abandoned task edits after save failures.
candidates.vertical_slice: Preserve the draft, explain the failure, and let the user retry or copy the content from the same editor.
candidates.duplication_check: The application has draft storage but no guided recovery experience connected to save failures.

candidates.title: First-Task Guided Tour
candidates.problem: New users may not understand how task assignment, due dates, and collaboration fit together.
candidates.measurable_outcome: Increase successful first-task completion during the first session.
candidates.vertical_slice: Show contextual guidance during the first task creation and dismiss it after completion.
candidates.duplication_check: No supplied evidence identifies an existing contextual onboarding flow.

candidates.title: Bulk Task Editing
candidates.problem: Users managing many tasks may repeat the same update across multiple records.
candidates.measurable_outcome: Reduce time spent applying shared task changes.
candidates.vertical_slice: Select multiple tasks and update one supported field with a review step.
candidates.duplication_check: No bulk-edit behavior appears in the supplied evidence, but repository verification is incomplete.

candidates.title: Workspace Activity Digest
candidates.problem: Returning collaborators may struggle to identify meaningful changes since their previous session.
candidates.measurable_outcome: Reduce time from return to first relevant action.
candidates.vertical_slice: Summarize assigned, mentioned, completed, and overdue changes in a dismissible panel.
candidates.duplication_check: No digest capability is present in the supplied evidence, but event availability requires verification.

candidates.title: Task Template Gallery
candidates.problem: Users may repeatedly recreate common task structures and checklists.
candidates.measurable_outcome: Reduce inputs required to create recurring task types.
candidates.vertical_slice: Offer a small user-owned template list at task creation with create, apply, and delete actions.
candidates.duplication_check: No template behavior is identified in the supplied evidence, but demand is unverified.

problem.statement: When saving a task fails, collaborators receive a generic error without knowing whether their draft is preserved or what action will safely complete the save.
jobs_to_be_done: Save task changes without losing entered content
Recover from a transient failure without restarting the workflow
current_friction: The failure message does not explain whether content is safe
The editor does not expose retry or copy actions next to the failure
Users may retype content or abandon the edit

outcomes.user_value: Users retain control of their work and can complete the save without re-entering task content.
outcomes.product_value: The product reduces abandonment and support burden while increasing trust in a core workflow.

metrics.name: Failed-save recovery rate
metrics.definition: Percentage of task save failures followed by a successful retry for the same draft within ten minutes.
metrics.target: Increase after rollout, with a target set after baseline collection
metrics.instrumentation: Correlate task_save_failed and task_save_recovered by anonymous draft attempt identifier.
metrics.guardrail: Do not increase duplicate task creation or repeated save requests.

metrics.name: Draft loss reports
metrics.definition: Count of support reports or in-product feedback claiming task content was lost after a save failure.
metrics.target: No increase and a downward trend after rollout
metrics.instrumentation: Tag supplied support reports with the save-recovery category.
metrics.guardrail: Investigate every confirmed content-loss case before expanding rollout.

scope.mvp: Preserve the current task draft after a failed save and present an inline recovery panel with retry, copy, and dismiss actions.
in_scope: Draft preservation for failed task creates and edits
Inline explanation of failure and draft safety
Retry, copy content, and dismiss controls
non_goals: Changing task permissions or ownership rules
assumptions: The local draft store can safely retain the current editor payload
Existing save errors can be normalized into retryable and non-retryable categories
constraints: Do not persist secrets or attachment binary data in local draft storage
Preserve current task authorization checks on every retry
The recovery UI must not imply success before server confirmation

primary_flow.action: Submits a valid task create or edit form.
primary_flow.system_response: The application attempts the save and keeps the current draft until confirmation.
primary_flow.success_condition: The task is saved and the temporary recovery draft is cleared.

primary_flow.system_response: The editor remains open and displays a recovery panel stating that the draft is preserved.
primary_flow.success_condition: The user can inspect the message and choose retry, copy, or dismiss without losing content.

primary_flow.action: Selects retry after connectivity or service availability improves.
primary_flow.system_response: The application resubmits the current draft once and shows progress without duplicating requests.
primary_flow.success_condition: The server confirms the save, the panel closes, and the draft is cleared.

alternate_flows.name: Non-retryable authorization failure
alternate_flows.trigger: The server rejects the save because the user no longer has permission.
steps: Keep the draft available for copying
Explain that retry cannot succeed without restored permission
Offer copy content and return to the task list
alternate_flows.resolution: The user retains the entered content without bypassing authorization.

alternate_flows.name: User dismisses recovery panel
alternate_flows.trigger: The user dismisses the inline recovery message while remaining in the editor.
steps: Keep the draft in the editor
Restore the panel after another failed save
alternate_flows.resolution: Dismissal does not discard the user's work.

states.trigger: The task editor is open and no save attempt is active.
states.experience: Show the editor normally with draft persistence available and no recovery panel.
states.recovery: No recovery action is needed until a save attempt fails.

states.trigger: The server confirms the task save.
states.experience: Show the existing success behavior and remove the recovery panel.
states.recovery: No recovery is required; clear the temporary draft only after confirmation.

states.trigger: A save or retry request is in flight.
states.experience: Disable duplicate submission while preserving navigation and show a labeled progress state.
states.recovery: Restore controls if the request fails or times out.

states.not_applicable_reason: The recovery panel is shown only for a populated task draft that has already passed client validation.

states.trigger: A retryable or non-retryable save failure occurs.
states.experience: Explain the failure category, confirm draft preservation, and expose only valid recovery actions.
states.recovery: Retry when allowed, copy content, or exit after acknowledging that the draft remains available.

states.trigger: Authorization is missing or revoked during save.
states.experience: State that the task cannot be saved with current access and avoid suggesting repeated retries.
states.recovery: Allow copying the draft and direct the user to request access through the existing permission path.

states.trigger: The user's session expires before the save request completes.
states.experience: Prompt sign-in, keep the current draft available, and block retry until reauthentication succeeds.
states.recovery: After sign-in, retry the save from the preserved draft.

states.trigger: The client detects no network connection during save.
states.experience: State that the draft is preserved locally and retry becomes available when connectivity returns.
states.recovery: Let the user retry manually after reconnection or copy the content immediately.

states.not_applicable_reason: The task save contract is atomic for the MVP, so the server cannot confirm only part of the task payload.

states.trigger: The save request is interrupted by navigation, tab closure, or an unexpected client shutdown.
states.experience: Keep the recovered draft locally and state that the save did not complete.
states.recovery: When the user returns, offer retry or copy from the preserved draft.

states.trigger: The user cancels an in-flight save or backs out before the request finishes.
states.experience: Stop the submission attempt, retain the draft, and confirm that nothing was sent.
states.recovery: Allow the user to continue editing and submit again later.

states.trigger: The server reports that the draft is based on an outdated task version.
states.experience: Explain that the remote task changed and keep the current draft visible for review or copy.
states.recovery: Let the user refresh the task state and reapply the desired edits manually.

accessibility.requirement: All recovery actions are keyboard reachable and focus moves to the recovery heading after a failed save.
accessibility.verification: Run the failure flow using only the keyboard and confirm focus restoration after retry.

accessibility.requirement: Failure, retry progress, and recovery success are announced without moving focus unexpectedly.
accessibility.verification: Verify live-region output with at least one supported screen reader.

accessibility.requirement: Failure category and action availability are not communicated by color alone.
accessibility.verification: Inspect the panel in normal, high-contrast, and grayscale conditions.

accessibility.requirement: The panel does not auto-dismiss and any progress animation respects reduced-motion preferences.
accessibility.verification: Enable reduced motion and confirm that recovery remains understandable and persistent.

content_guidance: Lead with whether the draft is safe before explaining the technical failure.
Use action labels that describe the result: Retry save, Copy task content, Dismiss message.
Do not expose raw server errors or imply that retry is guaranteed to succeed.
user_controls: Retry the save once per explicit activation
Dismiss the message without discarding the draft
Exit through the existing editor navigation after copying content

implementation.architecture_fit: Extend the existing task save error boundary and local draft store, keeping recovery state inside the task feature rather than introducing a global synchronization subsystem.

work_items.title: Normalize save failure contracts
work_items.objective: Expose retryability, permission status, and safe user-facing categories from task save failures.

paths.rationale: Centralizes deterministic mapping from transport errors to recovery behavior.
Represents the example task save boundary used by the recovery contract.

instructions: Define retryable, offline, permission, and terminal failure categories.
Keep raw error details out of user-facing output while preserving diagnostic logging.
Ensure retry requests retain existing authorization and idempotency behavior.
done_when: Every supported save error maps to one documented recovery category.
Unknown errors default to a safe terminal category without leaking raw details.

work_items.title: Persist recoverable task drafts
work_items.objective: Retain the current validated task draft until the server confirms a successful save.

paths.rationale: Provides the example repository seam for temporary recovery persistence.
Connects task editor lifecycle to temporary draft persistence.

instructions: Store only the validated text fields required to reconstruct the editor state.
Clear the temporary draft only after confirmed save or explicit discard.
Namespace drafts by workspace, user, and task or create-attempt identifier.
done_when: A failed save leaves the current draft available after the request completes.
A confirmed save clears only the matching temporary draft.

work_items.title: Build the recovery panel
work_items.objective: Present failure context and valid recovery actions without replacing the task editor.

paths.rationale: Contains the reusable, accessible recovery interaction.
Represents the example editor integration point.

instructions: Render copy, retry, and dismiss actions according to the normalized failure category.
Move focus to the panel heading after failure and restore focus after successful retry.
Keep the editor content and navigation available while the panel is visible.
done_when: The panel exposes only valid recovery actions for each failure category.
Keyboard and screen-reader checks pass for failure, retry, and success transitions.

work_items.title: Integrate recovery and instrumentation
work_items.objective: Connect draft persistence, recovery UI, analytics, and end-to-end verification in the task editor.

paths.rationale: Covers the integrated save and recovery state machine.
Verifies the user-visible vertical slice and regression behavior.

instructions: Correlate failure and recovery events with a non-sensitive attempt identifier.
Prevent duplicate retry submissions while a request is in flight.
Verify that permission failures never bypass authorization or expose retry when it cannot succeed.
done_when: All acceptance criteria pass in automated or documented manual verification.
Failure and recovery events contain no task content or personal data.
The feature can be disabled without affecting the existing save path.

data_changes: Add an expiring client-side recovery draft keyed to the current user and task attempt.
Do not add a server migration for the MVP.
api_changes: Normalize existing save failures in the client; no public API contract change is required for the MVP.

analytics_events.name: task_save_failed
analytics_events.trigger: A task save request returns a normalized failure category.
analytics_events.purpose: Establish failure volume and recovery funnel entry without recording task content.

analytics_events.name: task_save_recovery_action
analytics_events.trigger: The user activates retry, copy, or dismiss in the recovery panel.
analytics_events.purpose: Measure which recovery paths users choose.

analytics_events.name: task_save_recovered
analytics_events.trigger: A retry successfully saves the draft associated with a prior failure.
analytics_events.purpose: Calculate the primary failed-save recovery metric.

security_privacy: Never record task title, description, assignees, or attachment content in analytics.
Apply existing authorization checks to every retry request.
Expire temporary drafts and clear them after confirmed save or explicit discard.
Do not persist attachment binaries or credentials in local storage.

acceptance_criteria.given: A user submits a valid task and the save request fails with a retryable error
acceptance_criteria.then: The editor retains all entered fields and displays a recovery panel confirming that the draft is preserved
acceptance_criteria.verification: Automated integration test with a simulated retryable failure

acceptance_criteria.given: A retryable failure panel is visible and connectivity is available
acceptance_criteria.then: Exactly one save request is sent, progress is announced, and successful confirmation clears the temporary draft
acceptance_criteria.verification: End-to-end test with request counting and draft-store assertion

acceptance_criteria.given: A permission failure occurs while a populated task draft is open
acceptance_criteria.then: Retry is unavailable, the reason is explained, and the user can copy the draft without bypassing authorization
acceptance_criteria.verification: Integration test for permission category plus keyboard interaction check

acceptance_criteria.given: The recovery panel receives keyboard focus after a failed save
acceptance_criteria.when: The user navigates through its controls
acceptance_criteria.then: Focus order is logical, labels describe outcomes, and status changes are announced without an unexpected focus jump
acceptance_criteria.verification: Automated accessibility checks plus documented screen-reader smoke test

unit: Test mapping of retryable, offline, permission, terminal, and unknown errors.
Test temporary draft create, replace, expire, and clear behavior.
integration: Test editor content preservation across failed save and successful retry.
Test allowed actions for every normalized failure category.
end_to_end: Complete create and edit recovery flows with simulated failure followed by success.
Confirm a double activation cannot create duplicate save requests.
accessibility: Verify keyboard flow, focus placement, live announcements, non-color cues, and reduced motion.
manual: Verify copy behavior across supported browsers and confirm copied content excludes hidden metadata.
Inspect analytics payloads and local storage for prohibited task content.

phases: Enable for internal test workspaces and validate event payloads.
Enable for a small cohort after zero confirmed draft-loss regressions.
Expand after primary and guardrail metrics remain healthy.
rollback_triggers: Confirmed task content loss introduced by recovery handling
Duplicate task creation attributable to retry behavior
Authorization bypass or sensitive content in telemetry
rollback_plan: Restore the existing save error display while retaining diagnostic logs.
Preserve recoverable local drafts until users can copy or explicitly discard them.
observability: Track normalized failure counts, recovery actions, successful recoveries, and duplicate-save detection.
Alert on serialization errors in temporary draft storage.
Review support reports tagged as save recovery or content loss.

risks.risk: Retry behavior could create duplicate tasks if the original response is lost after a successful server write.
risks.mitigation: Reuse or add an idempotent attempt identifier and test ambiguous network completion.

risks.risk: Temporary draft persistence could retain sensitive task content longer than users expect.
risks.mitigation: Apply short expiration, explicit clearing, limited fields, and no analytics content capture.

open_questions.question: What expiration window matches the application's existing local draft policy?
open_questions.default_if_unanswered: Use the existing draft-store expiration; otherwise expire after twenty-four hours.

stop_conditions: Stop if the existing save API cannot support idempotent retry or distinguish ambiguous completion.
Stop if preserving the draft would store prohibited data or bypass the current security boundary.
Stop if repository evidence contradicts the assumed task editor or draft-store seams.
Stop if required tests, lint, type checks, or feature-contract validation fail.
completion_evidence: Passing unit, integration, end-to-end, accessibility, lint, and type-check commands.
Event payload inspection showing no task content or personal data.
Feature-flag disablement test proving the existing save path still works.
Acceptance-criteria matrix mapping every criterion to test evidence.
